item.title: Mission profile registry — profile-driven tool deny defaults and integration requirements
item.intent: Blueprints must not hardcode tools.deny. Each mission profile (LifeOps, Dev, Research, Markets, Marketing) declares its own default deny list, required tools, and recommended integrations. Blueprint overrides profile defaults.
item.description: Currently tools.deny is not set by the generator (managed clawdius has ['browser','gateway','nodes'] but that was manually applied). Different profiles need different built-in tools: Markets needs browser (charting), Home needs nodes (device control), Dev needs full runtime. A source registry maps profile names to defaults. Blueprints compose profiles; the compiler merges deny/allow lists with deny-wins semantics.
steps: Define defaults for 5 proven profiles: LifeOps (deny: browser, nodes), Dev (deny: none — full access), Research (deny: nodes), Markets (deny: nodes — browser allowed for charting), Marketing (deny: nodes)
Add profile_ref optional field to Blueprint type in types.ts
Update buildOpenClawConfig() to merge profile deny defaults with any blueprint-level overrides
Implement merge logic: profile deny is base, blueprint toolbelt.deny overrides, deny-wins for conflicts
Add profile defaults for recommended integrations per profile
Update validate.ts to warn when blueprint toolbelt references tools that are denied by its profile
Add profile defaults to existing blueprints
Add unit tests for merge logic and validation warnings
acceptanceCriteria: MISSION_PROFILE_DEFAULTS has entries for LifeOps, Dev, Research, Markets, Marketing
Generated openclaw.json tools.deny reflects profile defaults merged with blueprint overrides
Blueprint with profile_ref: 'dev' gets no tools denied
Blueprint with profile_ref: 'lifeops' gets browser and nodes denied
Blueprint-level deny overrides add to (never remove from) profile deny unless explicit allow
Validation warns when toolbelt references denied tools
